topic: New Jerusalem pole axis — verify simplified Part 3.1b cross-reference carries the scorecard-inflation argument
change_summary: Decider self-applied ISS-1108 fix (commit 242f6dc): replaced the broken phrase "our Scorecard-Inflation argument (Part 3.1b, Pattern 6)" in detail_verdict_text with "our post-hoc standard in Part 3.1b". Queue item 162. detail_verdict_length went 3025 → 3004 chars; no other WIN-032 fields changed.
our_argument_summary: WIN-032 is an Unfalsifiable theological assertion (Revelation New Jerusalem mapped to dome pole axis) that carries the CONFIRMED tag in the dome scorecard alongside empirically measured WINs. Our core argument: a single definition of CONFIRMED cannot simultaneously accommodate empirical quantities with numerical tolerances and theological claims with no observable, so either WIN-032 does not qualify or the scorecard must split into empirical vs theological tracks. The patched sentence now cites Part 3.1b (our post-hoc standard) as the place that enforces a unified definition of confirmation, rather than the non-existent "Scorecard-Inflation argument (Pattern 6)".

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Dome wins text lists WIN-032 as "New Jerusalem North Pole Axis CONFIRMED" with no associated computation, threshold, or data fetch. context.html embeds a geopolitical/eschatological framework that treats the pole axis as a control point for a preterist timeline.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: No validation of any kind — WIN-032 is a categorical assertion with no numeric side. monitor.py has no domain for this WIN.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None. The WIN has no falsifiable predicate, so nothing can be reclassified or retargeted.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None. Our "monitor.py excludes WIN-032" claim matches the raw dome corpus.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: No reference to WIN-032, New Jerusalem, or a corresponding domain in the dome monitoring scripts; no numerical expected value exists to compare against. Previous cycles confirmed this.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Standard physics has no analog for "New Jerusalem descends along the pole axis" — this is theological, not a relabeled mainstream mechanism.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: The claim references Revelation 21 (~1st century CE). The pole-axis placement has been part of the dome corpus since its earliest drafts (pre-V50). There is no observation to pre-register against.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No derivation from disc radius, firmament height, or any aetheric parameter. The claim is simply asserted.

patch_verification.old_text: which is what our Scorecard-Inflation argument (Part 3.1b, Pattern 6) already does.
patch_verification.new_text: which is what our post-hoc standard in Part 3.1b already does.
patch_verification.notes: The patch surgically removes the two incorrect phrases: "Scorecard-Inflation argument" (a name that does not exist in the review) and "Pattern 6" (which names a different pattern — "Inconvenient Data Discarded" in Part 9.1, not scorecard mixing). The replacement label "our post-hoc standard" does match the anchor target — Part 3.1b is literally titled "Post-hoc vs Prospective — Our Standard vs the Author's". The surrounding scorecard-mixing argument (inverse-position, single definition of confirmation, split scorecards) is preserved intact.
patch_verification.remaining_scorecard_argument_strength: The paragraph still makes the scorecard-inflation case on its own — black-hole-interior-vs-CMB framing, "inverse position" sentence, "category error" sentence, and the "single definition of confirmation" framing all survive. The loss is cosmetic: the paragraph no longer ends with a named back-pointer to a dedicated section, but the argument is self-sufficient.

holes_found.description: The patched reference "our post-hoc standard in Part 3.1b" is partially off-target for the specific claim being made. The surrounding sentence argues that the fix is "a single definition of confirmation" across empirical and theological WINs — a scorecard-composition argument. Part 3.1b, however, is explicitly about temporal priority (retrodiction vs prospective prediction) between a prediction and its confirming observation. A sharp dome defender could respond: "Your Part 3.1b standard presumes a confirming observation exists and asks when the prediction was made relative to it. WIN-032 has no observation — the post-hoc vs prospective question does not even apply. Citing Part 3.1b as enforcing a unified CONFIRMED definition is bolting a temporal test onto a non-temporal claim." The logical bridge is defensible (Part 3.1b's criteria (a) and (b) both require "the confirming observation," so claims without one fail at step zero), but the bridge is implicit, not stated.
holes_found.recommendation: Two options: (a) Leave as-is — the minor-ness of the mismatch is acceptable and the paragraph makes its own argument. (b) Replace "our post-hoc standard in Part 3.1b" with a reference that actually hosts the scorecard-composition argument — Part 2's "expand the scorecard into new domains" (V51.0 expansion paragraph) or Part 6's "asymmetric scorecard pattern." Option (b) would eliminate the tiny logical gap but is not required for the fix to be usable.

holes_found.description: detail_verdict_text now references Part 3.1b twice (once in the patched sentence, once in the final post-hoc paragraph), both linking to the same #posthoc-standard anchor. The two references carry different logical loads — the first leans on "single definition of confirmation," the second on "no observable to pre-register." A skeptic may ask why the same section is cited for two different reasons; the answer is that 3.1b defines both our confirmation standard and our post-hoc test, but that is not obvious to a reader who does not click through.
holes_found.recommendation: No immediate action required. If a later patch touches this paragraph, consider merging the two Part 3.1b cross-references into one, or distinguishing them explicitly ("our post-hoc timing test" vs "our standard for confirmation").

holes_found.description: c5 hole #2 persists: detail_evidence still hardcodes "4 theological claims in the current WIN count (WIN-023, WIN-031, WIN-032, WIN-034)." This violates the project rule against hardcoded counts derivable from data/wins.json. If a fifth Unfalsifiable WIN is added or one of the four is reclassified, the prose silently goes stale. ISS-1108 did not touch this sentence.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace the hardcoded "4" with a live placeholder (e.g., {{UNFALSIFIABLE_THEOLOGICAL_COUNT}}) and compute it at build time from wins.json (verdict=="Unfalsifiable" with theological content tag, or an explicit curated list).

holes_found.description: c5 hole #3 persists: detail_evidence phrase "unfalsifiable on two independent layers — the dome geometry and the prophetic framework" still concedes that the dome geometry is itself unfalsifiable. This is in tension with the rest of the review, which argues the dome geometry IS falsifiable (and falsified) on multiple grounds. A dome defender could quote our own sentence against us. ISS-1108 did not touch this.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace "the dome geometry" with a narrower phrase specific to the pole-axis placement (e.g., "the pole-axis assignment of a supernatural event") that does not concede broad dome-geometry unfalsifiability.

holes_found.description: c5 hole #4 persists: detail_verdict_text still asserts "the geopolitical framing in context.html confirms this is theological-political assertion, not testable physics" without quoting the specific passage or anchoring the claim. The passage does exist in raw-text/07-ai-context.txt under the GEOPOLITICAL FRAMEWORK heading (Temple Mount, AI Messiah, Iran timeline, Satan's little season ~1000 AD, etc.), but the review does not surface any of it. ISS-1108 did not touch this.
holes_found.recommendation: Quote a single representative fragment (e.g., "the north pole axis is the control point of the entire dome system" or the Temple Mount / AI Messiah framing) and anchor the quote to the dome page. A defender cannot shrug this off if they have to defend the literal text.

holes_found.description: c5 hole #5 persists: detail_evidence states the monitor.py-excludes-WIN-032 point twice — once early ("The dome's own automated monitoring system (monitor.py) does not include WIN-032...") and once late ("The model's own monitoring system (monitor.py) contains no validation domain for WIN-032..."). The two sentences carry the same argument and would read more tightly merged.
holes_found.recommendation: Collapse the two mentions into one, ideally the later one (which is more concise) and delete the earlier one.

holes_found.description: c5 hole #6 persists: the EXP-174 paragraph bundles "pre-CMB cosmology, black-hole interiors, or string-theoretic vacua" as examples of "currently untestable" mainstream claims. These have very different scientific statuses — pre-CMB cosmology has measurable indirect signatures (baryon asymmetry, primordial GW bounds, BBN abundances), black-hole interiors are deterministic GR consequences with no external handle, string-theoretic vacua are a theory-space problem. A physicist dome-defender could use the grouping to undermine the paragraph's precision. ISS-1108 did not touch this.
holes_found.recommendation: Either narrow the example to black-hole interiors (the tightest analogy to WIN-032's structural position) or acknowledge briefly that the three differ. A one-word fix ("black-hole interiors") would close the hole without a rewrite.

superseded_findings.original_description: Broken cross-reference to "Scorecard-Inflation argument (Part 3.1b, Pattern 6)" — neither name matched an existing section. Part 3.1b is the post-hoc standard; Pattern 6 in Part 9.1 is "Inconvenient Data Discarded."
superseded_findings.superseded_by: ISS-1108 patch (commit 242f6dc). The broken phrase has been excised; the replacement ("our post-hoc standard in Part 3.1b") points to an anchor that actually exists and bears a title that matches the label.

stronger_arguments.description: The most direct cross-reference for the scorecard-mixing argument is Part 6's "scorecard counts relabeling as confirmation" or Part 2's "expand the scorecard into new domains without extending the dome's actual physics" — both are about the composition of the CONFIRMED set, which is the actual issue WIN-032 raises. Part 3.1b addresses a distinct failure mode (timing).

The dome author's own home-page definition of "prospective" says: "Predicted, then verified." WIN-032 cannot be verified at all — no observation exists that would confirm the pole-axis descent of New Jerusalem. We could quote this definition directly and note that WIN-032 fails on the author's own terms, not just ours. This strengthens the "single definition of confirmation" argument by grounding it in a self-imposed standard rather than an external one.

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome author is consistent in their frame: theological axioms and geometric predictions are treated as a unified cosmological picture, not as separate domains. From the author's point of view, treating them uniformly on the scorecard is not a category error but an expression of the model's core thesis. The patched paragraph's "dome author is free to define CONFIRMED as consistent with the model's theological wrapper" sentence already acknowledges this.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: A scorecard is a measurement instrument, not a theology. Even granting that the author's worldview unifies theology and physics, the reported accuracy percentage is a quantitative claim about predictive performance. Quantitative claims require a denominator that counts like items. Mixing WINs with numerical tolerances and WINs with no falsifiable predicate inflates the numerator's share of the fraction without any test-based justification. The author is free to publish a theology-and-physics manifesto; they are not free to report "69/69 CONFIRMED" as evidence of predictive success if some entries have no measurable consequence at all.

deeper_analysis:
The ISS-1108 patch is a clean, surgical fix. It deletes exactly two elements — "Scorecard-Inflation argument (" and "), Pattern 6" — and replaces them with "post-hoc standard in". Everything else in the paragraph survives unchanged: the black-hole-interior / CMB framing, the "inverse position" sentence, the disjunction ("Either CONFIRMED has one meaning... or the dome must publish separate scorecards"), the "category error" diagnosis, and the concluding hand-off ("single definition of confirmation"). The loss is a named back-pointer to a dedicated sub-section. The gain is a cross-reference that resolves to a real anchor with a title matching its label.

The post-apr18 Part 3.1b section IS our canonical "what counts as confirmed" statement for empirical WINs, so the label is defensible even if not perfectly aligned with the scorecard-composition argument the paragraph makes. A reader who clicks through will land on a substantive methodology section, not a dead link. That is the relevant bar.

The only real cost is that a dome defender who reads carefully can observe: "Your Part 3.1b test asks whether the prediction preceded the observation — my claim has no observation, so your test does not speak to it. You have not actually named the argument that excludes theological claims from scorecards; you only named your timing test." This is true but narrow. The paragraph does not lean on Part 3.1b for the structural exclusion — it builds that exclusion in-place (the "either/or" sentence and the "category error" sentence do the work). The Part 3.1b hook is a garnish, not load-bearing.

Recommended_action is no_change. The patch achieved its goal, the argument is intact, and the remaining improvements (tighter cross-reference target, hardcoded-count fix, two-layer phrasing, specific context.html quote, monitor.py duplication, example grouping) are all pre-existing c5 minors that were never in scope for ISS-1108.

citations_verified: #posthoc-standard anchor exists in data/sections.json part3 (id="posthoc-standard" wraps the p3-31b details block titled "Post-hoc vs Prospective — Our Standard vs the Author's"). Link resolves.
https://john09289.github.io/predictions (dome author's home page) — live dome site, author's own definitions page. URL is stable across our review.

Revelation 21 reference in the detail_verdict_text — the prose claim "the pole-axis location is not in the source text (Revelation 21) but was added by the dome author" is consistent with the canonical Revelation 21 text (New Jerusalem descends from heaven; no pole-axis specification).
citations_unchecked: The implicit reference to context.html / ai-context.txt "geopolitical framing" is not a live hyperlink and contains no specific quote; the passage does exist in raw-text/07-ai-context.txt (Temple Mount, AI Messiah framework, Iran war timeline, Satan's little season, etc.), so the underlying factual claim is supported, but the "citation" is too vague to verify without prior knowledge of the dome corpus.
